stories.title: President Donald Trump visits Wheeler High in Cobb County | Live streaming coverage & updates
stories.summary: President Donald Trump visited Wheeler High School in Cobb County, Georgia, on July 22, 2026, to promote his Trump Accounts initiative, a new savings program for children that took effect on July 4. The event drew a crowd and included a speech by the president, who also called for passage of the "Save America Act." The visit caused rolling street closures as the president's motorcade temporarily disrupted traffic. The Cobb County School District said the visit showcased the district's academic excellence and high-achieving students.

stories.title: Commitment day: Five-star RB David Gabriel Georges, 2027's top uncommitted prospect, to announce on CBS Sports
stories.summary: Five-star running back David Gabriel Georges, the number one prospect in his home state and ranked 12th overall in the 2027 class, will announce his college commitment between Ohio State, Ole Miss and Tennessee on CBS Sports at 5:30 p.m. ET. The decision comes after official visits to all three finalists. Georges, a standout at Baylor School in Tennessee, rushed for 1,749 yards and 27 touchdowns last season. His choice will be live streamed on the CBS Sports College Football YouTube channel.

stories.title: Showers and isolated thunderstorms set to reignite with advancing cold front Wednesday
stories.summary: A cold front advancing into New England Wednesday will bring scattered showers and isolated thunderstorms, with Boston seeing the greatest chance of showers in the afternoon and evening. One or two of the storms could turn severe, posing a threat of strong, damaging wind gusts. The front is expected to bring dry air, dropping dew points from the 60s and 70s to the 50s by the afternoon. Residents can expect a return to comfortable humidity levels by nightfall.

stories.title: Why you won’t get a Supplemental Security Income check in August
stories.summary: Supplemental Security Income recipients will not receive their August payment on August 1, as the first day of the month falls on a Saturday. Instead, payments will be distributed on Friday, July 31. This is the fourth time in 2026 that SSI payments will be delayed due to a weekend or holiday. Beneficiaries will still receive their full monthly income, with subsequent payments scheduled for August 12, 19, and 26.

stories.title: Inter Miami under investigation after signing Casemiro
stories.summary: Major League Soccer is investigating Inter Miami for alleged tampering after the club signed Brazilian midfielder Casemiro on a deal through 2027 with an option to extend until 2029. The investigation centers on Casemiro's discovery rights, which the LA Galaxy held before Inter Miami acquired them. Inter Miami and the LA Galaxy have reached a settlement over the discovery priority. The outcome of the investigation will determine the validity of the settlement terms.

stories.title: Menzies taken ill on stage at World Matchplay
stories.summary: Scottish darts player Cameron Menzies was forced to forfeit his World Matchplay second-round match against Ross Smith in Blackpool on Wednesday after being taken ill on stage due to high blood pressure. Menzies was cleared by medics backstage but unable to return to the stage. He was deemed well enough to return to his accommodation. The match was forfeited, with world number three Gian van Veen advancing to the quarter-finals.

stories.title: IBM lowers full-year forecast after earnings warning
stories.summary: IBM has lowered its 2026 forecast, citing a 4% to 5% constant-currency revenue growth, down from its previous goal of over 5%. The company reported a 1% year-over-year revenue growth in the quarter, with net income decreasing to $2.17 billion. Shares rose 4% in extended trading. IBM is expected to deliver $1 billion in higher free cash flow for the year. The company's earnings warning last week prompted analysts to cut their estimates.

stories.title: Tesla’s revenues are bouncing back, but profits are still weak
stories.summary: Tesla reported a 25 percent increase in vehicle deliveries to 480,126 units in the second quarter of 2026, exceeding revenue expectations with $28.2 billion in revenue. The company's net income reached $1.11 billion, a 5 percent increase from the same period last year. Despite a revenue boost, Tesla's profits remain relatively weak. The company's focus on AI and robotics may be a long-term strategy, but its current financials are still largely driven by its automotive business.

stories.title: Brain injury cases should be thrown out, High Court told
stories.summary: A UK court has been told that 510 brain injury claims against rugby league and union bodies may be dismissed due to non-compliance with a court order. The claims involve 132 against rugby league bodies and 378 against rugby union bodies. The court is considering the severity of the breaches and potential consequences for the players. If the claims are dismissed, it could have significant implications for the future of contact sports in the UK.

stories.title: ‘Stuart Saves the Universe’ Producers on Pulling Off Such a Crazy ‘Big Bang Theory’ Spinoff After ‘No One Really Liked the Idea’
stories.summary: Chuck Lorre, creator of "The Big Bang Theory," pitched a spinoff featuring Stuart, played by Kevin Sussman, exploring a portal to other dimensions. However, the idea was met with rejection from network and studio executives who sought a more conventional spinoff with the show's main characters. Lorre wanted to try something entirely different and challenging. The spinoff, "Stuart Saves the Universe," is now in production.

stories.title: Nexstar Accused of Violating Injunction by Appointing Tegna Board
stories.summary: A coalition of 13 attorneys general and DirecTV accuse Nexstar of violating an injunction by appointing Tegna's board, allegedly undermining the TV station group's independence. The order, issued in April, requires Tegna to maintain separate operations from Nexstar. Nexstar has appointed its executives to serve on Tegna's board, despite the plaintiffs' claims this is not allowed. The case will now be taken to Judge Troy Nunley for clarification.

stories.title: ‘Pompeii: Out of Time with Tom Hiddleston’: A Rare Doc That Could Actually Attract a Young Audience
stories.summary: Tom Hiddleston stars in a three-part documentary series, "Pompeii: Out of Time with Tom Hiddleston," examining the 79 AD eruption of Mount Vesuvius and the survival attempts of three real Romans. The Nat Geo docuseries blends time travel, scripted drama, and interviews with experts. The series challenges long-held assumptions about the disaster, using ancient records and artifacts to bring Pompeii's inhabitants to life. It premieres in 2024, aiming to attract a wider audience, including younger viewers.

stories.title: US to announce deal allowing Saudi Arabia a nuclear programme
stories.summary: US President's administration is expected to announce a deal allowing Saudi Arabia to develop a civilian nuclear program, potentially worth billions of dollars to US companies. The agreement would enable Saudi Arabia to enrich uranium on its soil, producing fuel at a US-built facility. This move has raised concerns among nuclear experts and former Israeli officials. The White House has not commented on the deal, which would require Congressional consultation.

stories.title: Price-hiked iPads are a little cheaper right now
stories.summary: A premium 13-inch iPad Pro with 512GB of storage is currently discounted by $150 at Amazon and $100 at Best Buy, bringing its price to $1,549.99. The deal applies to the M5 model with impressive performance and features. This is the most expensive iPad Apple sells. The discount is available now, but it's unclear how long it will last.

stories.title: Coveted U.S. Nuclear Deal Is a Coup for Saudi Crown Prince
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia had for years sought the controversial deal to obtain American nuclear technology. It is expected to be sealed on Wednesday, deepening U.S.-Saudi ties for decades to come.

stories.title: U.S.-Saudi Nuclear Deal Stokes Israeli Concerns of Mideast Arms Race
stories.summary: An Israeli minister sought to downplay the agreement, while critics said it could spur other regional countries to pursue a nuclear program, and potentially the creation of a bomb.

stories.title: Canadian Wildfire Evacuees Outraged Over Lacking Support and Communication
stories.summary: Residents of northwestern Ontario say their warnings about fires they spotted were dismissed and official information as the flames spread often didn’t come at all.

stories.title: Alphabet Quadruples Profit to Nearly $120 Billion, Fueled by A.I. Investments
stories.summary: Google’s parent company, which has spent heavily on artificial intelligence, also showed growth across its businesses, particularly in cloud computing.

stories.title: House Passes Defense Bill Amid Iran War Divide
stories.summary: Republicans pushed through the $1.15 trillion bill over opposition from Democrats, who said backing it was akin to embracing the war.

stories.title: Police formally investigate woman after Brit stabbed to death in French village
stories.summary: A 70-year-old woman has been formally investigated in connection with the 2023 stabbing death of 65-year-old British-South African mother Karen Carter in Trémolat, France. Carter was found with stab wounds near her car by a friend in the Dordogne village where she lived. Prosecutors say the woman was initially taken into custody in 2025 as part of preliminary inquiries before being released. The case will now proceed to trial if the investigating judge finds sufficient evidence.

stories.title: Trump’s 50% Tariffs on Canada: What to Know, and What’s Next
stories.summary: The president said punishing 50 percent tariffs would begin by Aug. 19, a significant escalation of the trade war between Canada and the U.S. that he started over a year ago.

stories.title: Speaking for Fallen Soldiers, Trump Says They Supported the Iran War
stories.summary: President Trump attended a dignified transfer for the soldiers as Americans were growing increasingly critical of the war.

stories.title: Trump threatens to target Iran's bridges and power plants if Hormuz attacks persist
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump has threatened to destroy Iranian bridges and power plants if attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz continue. The potential targets include structures in or near Tehran, the capital city. This warning comes after 11 consecutive nights of US strikes on Iran and Iranian warnings of reprisals. The situation remains volatile with multiple reports of explosions in Iranian cities.

stories.title: NASA to Showcase Agency’s Newest Wind Tunnel in Virginia
stories.summary: NASA will showcase its newest wind tunnel, the Flight Dynamics Research Facility, at the Langley Research Center in Hampton, Virginia, on July 31. The facility, the agency's first new wind tunnel in over 40 years, will be the site of a ribbon-cutting ceremony and media tour. NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman and other officials will be available for media questions. The event is open to US citizens and lawful permanent residents, with RSVPs due by July 29.

stories.title: What Were the Lotus-Eaters Eating?
stories.summary: In Homer’s epic, Odysseus’s men eat something so powerful that they forget about home. Which plants could have produced such a soporific, narcotic and amnesiac effect?

stories.title: Donald Trump Jr.’s Investment Firm Posts Staggering Returns of 200%
stories.summary: The fledgling firm, run by the president’s oldest son and his partner Omeed Malik, has made investments in A.I. and defense technology that have tripled in value in just months.

stories.title: Global Oil Price Jumps as War Threats Intensify
stories.summary: But stock investors shrug off Iran developments as they await crucial earnings reports from tech firms including Alphabet and Tesla.

stories.title: Target ultra-processed food to reduce obesity in Britain | Letter
stories.summary: A British health select committee report has found that ultra-processed foods contribute to obesity, with sugar and refined carbohydrates identified as primary drivers of weight gain and type 2 diabetes. The report recommends targeting supermarkets and manufacturers to reduce obesity in Britain. The committee's findings are based on evidence that the current "high in fat, salt or sugar" framework is flawed. The government is expected to implement new policies to address the issue.

stories.title: Government Overhauls Teen Pregnancy Program to Focus on Marriage and Starting Families
stories.summary: The administration canceled federal grants to organizations across the country, accusing them of encouraging premarital sex. New criteria say programs should stress abstinence.
